scope: round-4 A1 gate-2 (2x2: init x edit-weight) + gate-1 architecture-matched floor + reconstruction twin, before write-up
process: 6 dimension skeptics -> per-finding independent verification (re-derived from artifacts/code)

major_confirmed.title: Pretrained-vs-randinit error deltas are NOT small and are highly significant at ew10; 'small' is an overstatement
major_confirmed.corrected_statement: The finding is confirmed. Recomputing pretrained-minus-randinit from the five aggregate JSONs (population std, two-sample z with n=5): ew0 error deltas state -0.62 (z-1.25, genuinely noisy), noop -0.69 (z-9.0), invalid -2.30 (z-17.8, 29.3% lower haze); ew10 error deltas state -3.10 (z-8.2), noop -1.72 (z-9.6), invalid -5.24 (z-12.0, 23.5% lower invalid haze). AUC delta -0.085 (z-8.0) ew0 / -0.011 (z-1.7) ew10; rank_vs_random -0.040 (z-3.4) / -0.028 (z-2.8). Direction is correct (error favors pretrained, AUC/rank favor randinit) but 'small' understates the ew10 error effect and the ew0 AUC effect. Both init modes fail every clause: their state-changing h1 (66-72) is worse than the identity copy floor (63.13, runs/gate2/round4-copyfloor-hardened.json) and noop/invalid haze is positive against a 0 copy floor, so no clause verdict flips (both ALL-FAIL). Adopt the suggested correction: 'no clause verdict flips between init modes (both ALL-FAIL), so pretraining is verdict-irrelevant; however pretraining reproducibly and significantly REDUCES feature-space haze on the error readouts (invalid-haze ~24-29% lower, state-chg -3.1 at ew10, all |z|>8 across the 5 seeds) while randinit yields modestly higher AUC/rank; the inits trade error-magnitude vs discrimination and neither reaches a pass.' Note the |z| values reflect tight seed-to-seed variance (reproducibility), not large absolute effects, and are specific to the frozen-ModernBERT 301x768 feature-space configuration.

major_confirmed.title: Recon's macro edge over frozen is ENTIRELY cwd; on both filesystem-state readouts recon is WORSE than frozen
major_confirmed.corrected_statement: CONFIRMED. Pooled linear macro (probes/probe.py:158 = (cwd+exists_balacc+cls_given_exists)/3): recon 0.5424, frozen 0.4441, JEPA/sigreg 0.2603. The recon-vs-frozen +9.84pt macro gap decomposes EXACTLY into cwd +0.4756, exists -0.1367, cls -0.0438 (sum/3 = +0.0984). On both filesystem-state readouts recon is WORSE than frozen; recon cls_given_exists (0.1173) is below the majority baseline (0.17483) and exists_balacc (0.578) is barely above chance (0.5). The recon-vs-JEPA gap is likewise 98.3% cwd (cwd delta +0.8324 vs exists +0.0127, cls +0.0013); JEPA cwd 0.0996 is at/below its chance floor 0.1028. Recommended RQ1 rewrite: "The recon twin recovers cwd (0.93) whereas frozen (0.46) and JEPA (0.10, ~chance) do not; on file existence and content-class both recon and frozen are near-chance and recon is if anything slightly worse than frozen." Do NOT claim generative reconstruction preserves more transferable file-state; it preserves a single positional readout (cwd). Caveat the write-up must include: (1) this is a single-seed (s0) recon/JEPA twin comparison, so it is descriptive attribution, not a multi-seed verdict; (2) frozen's own gate-1 clause still fails independently (44.41 < floor+20 = 50.63 and < recon+10 = 64.24), so nothing here rehabilitates the frozen pooled path.

major_confirmed.title: "5-seed aggregate" is a single-training-seed result — the multi-seed-for-verdicts rule is NOT satisfied and this must be stated in the CLAIM A headline, not buried
major_confirmed.corrected_statement: CONFIRMED. The round-4 '5-seed aggregate' is a single-training-seed diagnostic: all four trained arms (runs/gate2/round4-{pretrained,randinit,pretrained-ew10,randinit-ew10}/ckpt.pt) were trained ONCE at train_args.seed=0, steps=3000 (differing only in trunk_init and edit_weight). The battery's --seeds 0,1,2,3,4 vary only eval-time sampling: evals/dynamics.py constructs the adapter+ckpt once (line 397) then loops seeds re-seeding only random.Random('dynamics:{seed}') (line 319), and every per_seed entry stamps the same ckpt (line 411) — the distinct-ckpt set is one path per arm. Hence all reported std bands (e.g. state-chg 66.19+/-0.81, AUC 0.784+/-0.016) are eval-sampling variance around a single trained network, NOT training-run variance, which §7 (terminal-jepa.md:76) names as the dominant source and the primary seed unit. The pre-registered >=5-seed / 5-TRAINING-seed rule and 'multi-seed for verdicts' standing rule are therefore UNMET, as status line 136 already concedes. Required write-up corrections: (1) relabel every band as eval-sampling variance over one trained checkpoint per arm; (2) state in the CLAIM A headline (not buried) that this is a single-training-seed diagnostic with §7's 5-training-seed bar unmet; (3) the FAIL direction is robust to this (no bad draw can fake all-4-arms/all-5-eval-seeds failing the no-op~0, invalid~0, AUC>=0.9 clauses), but the pretrained-vs-randinit deltas are single-training-seed and in fact favor randinit (ew0: state-chg -0.62, AUC -0.085; ew10: state-chg -3.10, AUC -0.011), so any 'pretraining helps/does not help' claim could flip under retraining and must not be stated at rule level; (4) cite the handoff's own admission (status line 136). Note only that 'CLAIM A / 5-seed aggregate / clean FAIL' is the reviewed write-up's phrasing, not text currently in the committed docs.

major_confirmed.title: "Recipe-faithful" overstates fidelity to VL-JEPA: 15.9M predictor is 1/30th of VL-JEPA's 490M, uses only 3 of ModernBERT's 22 layers, and stubs the token table — disclose all three
major_confirmed.corrected_statement:
CONFIRMED as written, with two minor precisions the write-up should carry:

(1) The '94.7% / 15.05M' block (trunk_lm = 15,047,424) is the 3 reused layers PLUS 2,304 residual params (the 1-row stub tok_embeddings, embeddings.norm, and final_norm, all 768-vectors). The three attention/MLP layers proper are 3 × 5,015,040 = 15,045,120 (94.70%). Both round to the finding's figures, but if the write-up decomposes to the last dollar it should say '15.05M (3 truncated layers + trunk norms/stub)', not '15.05M = 3 layers' — otherwise 3 × 5,015,040 = 15,045,120 ≠ 15,047,424 looks like a 2,304-param arithmetic gap.

(2) Scale confound is even sharper than stated against the plan's OWN spec: terminal-jepa.md:42 specs the predictor at '~5-10M params', so 15.9M already exceeds the predictor-line spec even while sitting under the ≤20M *total* cap (terminal-jepa.md:45). The suggested correction — replace bare 'recipe-faithful' with 'recipe-faithful within the project's ≤20M-param envelope (terminal-jepa.md:45), ~1/30th of VL-JEPA's 490M predictor', and disclose that only 3 of 22 layers are reused and the token table is stubbed so scale AND depth remain confounds — is adopted. The delta head is nn.Linear(768→768)=590,592 (not d→FEAT_D as a distinct projection); this does not change any number.

major_confirmed.title: CLAIM A over-generalizes: the arms show real above-chance dynamics (goal-ranking 0.77–0.87) — they are NOT copy-equivalent failures, and 'the recipe fails' is broader than what feature-space L2 measures
major_confirmed.corrected_statement: CLAIM A's gate verdict is correct (all four arms fail the three pre-registered GATE clauses: state-changing h1 mean worse than the copy floor 63.13 by +3.07 to +9.06; nonzero no-op/invalid haze vs copy's 0; max AUC 0.8999 < 0.9), but its framing over-generalizes. Scope it to 'a small (15.9M) predictor doing feature-space-L2 regression over frozen ModernBERT path-keyed features hazes on the calibration clauses' — NOT 'the recipe fails.' The feature-space-L2 distance (models/gate2.py:499, summed over 301x768 dims) is a different metric and unit from the symbolic edit-count reference (1.076 copy / 0.277 symbolic pipeline; evals/dynamics.py:69-73), so those numbers are not directly comparable. Report the above-chance goal-distance ranking (rank-vs-random 0.77-0.87, rank-vs-invalid 0.69-0.87, both consistent across all four predicate kinds) vs the copy floor's definitional 0.500 as a partial positive so the arms are not mischaracterized as copy-equivalent, and note explicitly that (a) the copy-floor 0.500 on ranking/AUC and 0 on no-op/invalid are mechanical artifacts of predict=identity (CopyDynamics, dynamics.py:88-89), so 'no arm beats copy' concerns only the state-changing-error axis, and (b) the ranking signal is led by the RANDOM-INIT trunk (0.867 vs pretrained 0.827), so it evidences a live representation interface, not a benefit of pretraining.

major_confirmed.title: CLAIM C's 'reconstruction preserves more transferable pooled state' is an artifact of cwd: the recon twin decodes CONTENT below chance, and the pooled-floor comparison is not dimension-matched
major_confirmed.corrected_statement: CONFIRMED. Independently re-derived: recon-twin pooled linear macro = (cwd 0.9320 + exists_balacc 0.5780 + content_given_exists 0.11732)/3 = 0.542434 (probes/probe.py:158; runs/recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json:24-27), matching the JSON exactly. The macro is an equal-weight 3-way mean, and cwd (a 1-of-~10 classification, floor 10.28%) is the only meaningful positive signal: content-given-exists 11.73% is 5.75 pts BELOW the 17.48% majority-class floor (probe-v2.json:19), and exists_balacc 57.8% is barely above its 0.5 chance floor. So the recon twin preserves the cwd initial-state scalar while DESTROYING content decodability — the same layout-entanglement pattern as the JEPA arms, not 'more transferable state.' The +10-over-twin FAIL is arithmetically correct (frozen pooled macro = (0.4564+0.71467+0.16109)/3 = 0.4441 = 44.41% < twin+10 = 64.24%; runs/frozen-modernbert-v1/probe.json:26-29) and directionally robust, BUT the clause is dimension-mismatched: frozen encoder_d_out=768 (probe.json:23) vs recon twin encoder_d_out=256 (probe-v2.json:22). The recon-twin-vs-JEPA-arm leg is matched on the recon side (ckpt.pt: arm=recon, encoder_type=cls, seed=0, steps=4000, data=data/v1). Suggested wording stands: 'the recon twin's higher pooled macro is carried by cwd (93.2%); its content-given-exists (11.7%) is below the 17.5% floor, so it does not preserve transferable CONTENT — it preserves the cwd scalar,' and flag the 768-d-vs-256-d dimension mismatch of the +10-over-twin clause. Two reviewer notes the write-up must carry: (a) 'carried almost entirely by cwd' is if anything an understatement — in above-floor terms cwd contributes +82.9 pts while content is -5.8 pts and exists +7.8 pts; and (b) I could not verify the handoff 'line 133' claim or the JEPA-arm 26-28% figures because terminal-jepa-status.md and the JEPA-arm probe artifact are not present in this repo (only README.md and the recon/frozen artifacts), so those two items rest on the write-up's own record, not on artifacts I recomputed.

sound_and_minor.title: Copy floor and all arms use the identical feature-space L2 distance - same unit confirmed
sound_and_minor.corrected: Finding confirmed. Copy floor (63.13) and all four learned round-4 arms use the identical feature-space L2 distance (Gate2Adapter.distance, models/gate2.py:499-500: per-slot L2 over 768 dims summed over 301 slots) and the identical encode(); they differ only in predict() (CopyDynamics identity vs the learned net). Same unit, same representation, architecture-matched — 63.13 is the correct and only valid baseline for the 66-72 arm numbers. Hand-check reproduced (30.712812 == 3+sqrt(768)); all five aggregate arm numbers re-derived from the JSONs match the dossier; per-seed copyfloor is exactly noop=0/inv=0/auc=0.5 on all 5 seeds, and the AUC=0.5 is correct (calibration measures predicted-change magnitude, which is 0 for the identity predictor, so it sits at chance — the intended floor behavior). Aggregation is mean-based over both samples and seeds (dynamics.py:338-341, 365), satisfying the strict mean-scoring rule. The symbolic reference numbers (oracle-copy 1.0763, tier2 0.2766) come from OracleAdapter's integer edit-count distance (dynamics.py:69-73), a different metric on a ~1-unit scale, and must never be presented as comparable to the feature-space L2 numbers. Two immaterial citation nits: (1) the copyfloor command is at runs/gate2/run_round4.sh lines 14-17 (not 15-16); (2) the JSON records the copyfloor adapter name as 'gate2-clean-copy'. Neither affects the finding.

sound_and_minor.title: copy no-op/invalid = 0 is a correct consequence of clean regime + state-invariance, not a bug flattering copy
sound_and_minor.corrected: Finding confirmed as stated, with the offered caveat already correct and necessary. Copy predictor h1 error on no-op/invalid transitions is EXACTLY 0.0 (not approximately) in the clean input regime because: (1) no-op and invalid transitions are provably state-invariant (env/actions.py:63 types by state delta; invalid _err returns the unmodified state), and (2) clean render_full(state,None,None) is a pure function of state alone, so state[t+1]==state[t] yields byte-identical text and the frozen deterministic ModernBERT encoder produces identical features, giving feature-space-L2 distance 0. Re-derived directly: traj-0 has noop=2/invalid=2/state-changing=20 with 0 state inequalities and 0 clean-render mismatches on no-op/invalid; across all 200 battery-pool trajs, 1320/1320 no-op/invalid transitions are render-identical (copy=0) and 3480/3480 state-changing are render-different (copy>0); running the real CopyDynamics(Gate2Adapter(clean)) gives distance 0.0 on every traj-0 no-op/invalid and 13.28 on a state-changing transition. Copyfloor aggregate (63.127/0.0/0.0/0.500) and all four arm rows reproduce to stated precision; copy and arms share the identical clean-regime feature-space-L2 referee (architecture-matched, same-unit). The write-up must retain the finding's own caveat: copy=0 on no-op/invalid is specific to --input-regime clean (input render == target render); under --input-regime both the nuisance-carrying input render differs from the clean target even on no-ops, so copy no-op would be nonzero. This is the correct calibration bar (dynamics.py:78-79), not a metric quirk or a bug flattering copy.

sound_and_minor.title: Every arm exceeds copy on state-changing at every seed - claim holds under strict MEAN scoring and per-seed
sound_and_minor.corrected: Finding CONFIRMED as sound. Under strict MEAN scoring, all four learned gate-2 arms have higher state-changing h1 feature-space error than the copy floor (copy 63.13 vs 66.19/66.81/69.09/72.18), and this holds per-seed: each arm's minimum state-changing seed (64.92/65.68/68.27/71.80) exceeds copy's maximum (64.50), so arm-min > copy-max for every arm — stronger than the mean comparison. AUC global max across all seeds/arms = 0.8999 (rand-ew10 seed1), never reaching 0.9. Copy's 0-error on no-op/invalid and AUC=0.5 are structural (no-op/invalid leave state unchanged: 604/604, 716/716; state-changing always changes: 3480/3480), so copy is exactly optimal on non-state-changing and is the correct same-unit floor to beat on state-changing; the arms fail to beat it and add error relative to copying. Distance is summed feature-space L2 over [301,768] ModernBERT slot features (models/gate2.py:499), NOT symbolic edit count — the reference symbolic numbers (copy 1.076, tier2 0.277) use OracleAdapter's edit-count distance and are a different, non-comparable unit. Required caveats for the write-up: (1) keep the symbolic reference numbers strictly segregated as a different metric and never imply the gate2 arms 'should' approach 0.277; (2) state the claim precisely — copy is the least-error DO-NOTHING (identity) predictor and is provably optimal on non-state-changing transitions, but it is not unbeatable in principle on state-changing (the symbolic tier2 pipeline beats its own copy floor, 0.277 < 1.076); the finding correctly claims only that these particular frozen-feature gate-2 arms fail to beat it, with no generalization beyond this tested configuration (data/v1, held-out split, 5 seeds).

sound_and_minor.title: pretrained-vs-randinit deltas are verdict-irrelevant (correct) but not uniformly 'small' - ew10 invalid/state-changing deltas are sizeable
sound_and_minor.corrected: Confirmed. Feature-space L2 distance (models/gate2.py:500), same units across the copy floor and all arms, so % -of-copy-floor framing is valid; means used for all clause verdicts. pretrained-minus-randinit deltas — ew0 {state-chg -0.62, noop -0.69, invalid -2.30, AUC -0.085}; ew10 {state-chg -3.10, noop -1.72, invalid -5.24, AUC -0.011}; rank_acc_vs_random favors randinit (ew0 .827 vs .867; ew10 .774 vs .802) — are all verdict-irrelevant: every arm's state-changing mean stays ABOVE the copy floor (63.127) and no AUC reaches 0.9, so no clause flips. But 'small' understates the ew10 error deltas: invalid haze drops 23.5% relative (17.02 vs 22.26; 8.3% of the copy floor) and state-changing haze drops 4.9% of the copy floor, both exceeding 1 per-seed std of either arm. Reword to: 'deltas are verdict-irrelevant (no clause changes); error metrics favor pretrained and AUC/rank favor randinit, the largest single effect being ~24% lower invalid-haze for pretrained at edit-weight 10 — still far from the copy floor and clause thresholds.' Avoid calling all deltas uniformly 'small.'

sound_and_minor.title: edit-weight 10 raises no-op/invalid haze for both inits; reference symbolic floor confirmed as a different metric
sound_and_minor.corrected: No correction needed. Finding is confirmed. Keep the explicit statement that the feature-space copy floor (state-changing 63.13, an L2 summed over 301 slots x 768 dims per Gate2Adapter.distance / CopyDynamics delegation) and the symbolic edit-count reference (oracle-copy 1.076, tier2 0.277 per OracleAdapter.distance) are incommensurable (~59x scale gap), and that only the same-unit 63.13 feature-space floor gates the round-4 arms. Edit-weight 10 raises no-op haze (pt 5.10->10.33, ri 5.79->12.05), invalid haze (pt 5.55->17.02, ri 7.85->22.26), and state-changing error (pt 66.19->69.09, ri 66.81->72.18) for both inits. Recommend the write-up add: results are scoped to the tested clean-input frozen-ModernBERT 301-slot config (no generalization) and are 5-seed means.

sound_and_minor.title: All 4 JEPA arms fail calibration (noop!=0, invalid!=0, AUC<0.9) - confirmed, with a copy-floor caveat
sound_and_minor.corrected: CONFIRMED (with a minor rounding note). All four JEPA arms fail dynamics-gate calibration on held-out val, 5-seed means: pretrained_ew0 noop=5.10/invalid=5.55/AUC=0.784; randinit_ew0 5.79/7.85/0.869; pretrained_ew10 10.33/17.02/0.869; randinit_ew10 12.05/22.26/0.879. Every arm has nonzero no-op and invalid h1 haze and aggregate AUC below the 0.9 pass bar (max aggregate 0.8792). The best PER-SEED AUC is 0.89987 (randinit_ew10 seed 1) — below 0.9, so AUC never reaches the bar though it grazes it; state this as 0.89987 rather than "0.8999" for precision. All four arms correctly perturb invalid latents more than no-op latents (invalid>noop everywhere). REQUIRED CAVEAT (verified): the copy floor's 0.00 no-op/invalid rollout haze and 0.500 AUC are degenerate identity artifacts — the copy predictor moves nothing, so (a) for no-op/invalid transitions the true next state equals the anchor, giving 0 rollout error trivially, and (b) all calibration change-magnitudes are exactly 0.0 (all 5 seeds), so the state-changing-vs-non AUC is an all-ties 0.5 floor, not a learned discrimination. The correct comparison is therefore "JEPA AUC 0.78-0.88 sits above the tie floor but below the 0.9 pass bar," and the JEPA predictor demonstrably perturbs latents even when the ground truth is unchanged (that is the calibration haze the clause exists to catch). The distance metric is feature-space L2 summed over 301 slots x 768 dims (models/gate2.py:499-500) and is not comparable to the symbolic edit-count references.

sound_and_minor.title: Edit-weight ew0->ew10 monotonically increases all haze/error metrics for both inits - confirmed, with an AUC nuance
sound_and_minor.corrected:
CONFIRMED (with one AUC qualification). Higher edit-weight (ew0->ew10) monotonically inflates all haze/error metrics for BOTH inits, and the effect is robust: every one of the 5 seeds increases on no-op, invalid, and state-changing h1 error for both pretrained and randinit (not just at the mean). Recomputed deltas match the finding exactly - pretrained: noop +5.2299, invalid +11.4725, state-chg +2.8930; randinit: noop +6.2596, invalid +14.4106, state-chg +5.3716. Metric is feature-space L2 summed over 301x768 (gate2.py:499-500), aggregates are true 5-seed means (MEAN rule respected), no learned arm beats the copy floor on state-changing (all 66-72 > copy 63.13) nor matches copy's 0.0 on no-op/invalid, so 'neither ew setting passes anything' holds.

The AUC nuance needs tightening: append 'Note ew10 raises AUC even as it inflates all error/haze magnitudes, so the edit-weight effect is a haze-vs-discrimination trade. This AUC gain is solid for the PRETRAINED arm (0.784->0.869; +0.0845, paired t=7.27, df=4, p<0.01, all 5 seeds up) but is WITHIN NOISE for randinit (0.869->0.879; +0.0103, paired t=0.79, n.s.; one seed regressed -0.0208, one flat). Do not claim the AUC improvement holds for both inits.' The finding's *suggested correction wording* is acceptable because it illustrates the AUC gain using the pretrained number, which is real; but the finding's body phrasing 'randinit 0.869->0.879' should carry the n.s. caveat.

sound_and_minor.title: No JEPA arm beats the feature-space copy floor on state-changing error - confirmed, matched-unit baseline
sound_and_minor.corrected: CONFIRMED (matched-unit, matched-metric, multi-seed-robust): No JEPA arm beats the feature-space copy floor 63.13 on state-changing error. Copy floor state_changing_h1_mean = 63.1269 (mean of per-seed [61.78, 63.01, 64.50, 63.28, 63.06]); all four arms strictly worse: pretrained_ew0 66.19 (+3.07), randinit_ew0 66.81 (+3.68), pretrained_ew10 69.09 (+5.96), randinit_ew10 72.18 (+9.06). No per-seed overlap: JEPA per-seed min 64.92 (pretrained_ew0 seed0) > copy floor per-seed max 64.50. The copy floor uses the SAME Gate2Adapter encode() and the SAME distance() = (a-b).norm(dim=-1).sum() over 301x768 slots, differing only in predict=identity (evals/dynamics.py:76-92; models/gate2.py:499-500), so this is a genuine like-for-like comparison. The write-up must add three caveats: (1) copy's noop=0.00 / invalid=0.00 / AUC=0.500 are definitional artifacts, not empirical wins — copy predict=identity gives distance(z,z)=0 for no-op/invalid where the clean-rendered target equals the input, and AUC=0.5 follows from all magnitudes tying at 0 under the tie rule; (2) the copy floor is ckpt-invariant (predict ignores the net, encode uses only the frozen LM, distance is pure tensor math), so loading round4-pretrained/ckpt.pt for the copy run does not make it 'pretrained-specific'; (3) the claim is scoped strictly to the state-changing clause — on invalid/no-op separation, calibration AUC (0.78-0.88), and goal-ranking, the JEPA arms clearly beat copy's 0.5, so 'worse than copy' must not be generalized to 'worse overall.'

sound_and_minor.title: Cross-unit non-comparability caveat is warranted, but the causal 'decode bottleneck carries the pass' clause is an interpretation, not a recomputed round-4 result
sound_and_minor.corrected: The finding is correct as written and needs no correction. Both parts hold: (1) the cross-unit non-comparability caveat is warranted — the five round-4 arms report FEATURE-SPACE L2 (models/gate2.py:499-500, (a-b).norm(dim=-1).sum() over 301 slots x 768 dims), scoring 63-72, versus the symbolic edit-count reference numbers oracle-copy 1.0763 and tier2 slotpred-v2 0.2766, a ~59-67x scale gap, so "NOT directly comparable" is required; and (2) the causal clause "the symbolic decode bottleneck CARRIES finding-17's gate-2 pass" is an interpretation, not a recomputed round-4 result — the five round-4 JSONs contain no reference to finding-17 or the decoder and demonstrate only that a learned predictor over frozen features fails the dynamics gate in feature space (both inits: noop 5.1-12.0, invalid 5.5-22.3 over the copy-floor 0; AUC 0.78-0.88 vs copyfloor 0.5). Adopt the finding's suggested wording: downgrade "the symbolic decode bottleneck CARRIES finding-17's pass" to "consistent with the hypothesis that finding-17's pass was driven by the symbolic decode/codebook stage (Gate2CodebookAdapter -> tier-2 symbolic predictor) rather than frozen-feature dynamics," and note this inference rests on the tier2/codebook runs (a different metric and different runs), not on these five round-4 attribution JSONs. Add one caveat the finding omits: the status docs asserting the attacked claim are not in this checkout, so the verification is against the artifacts and code, not the doc's exact phrasing.

sound_and_minor.title: Clean-regime encode is ctx-invariant: finding-19 exemplar/state offset does NOT bite the reported numbers
sound_and_minor.corrected: No correction needed; the finding is sound as stated. The write-up must retain the caveat exactly as offered: the exemplar-vs-state ctx asymmetry (dynamics.py:266-267 encode with ctx=None vs line 270 encode with _ctx) is harmless ONLY because --input-regime clean makes Gate2Adapter.encode (gate2.py:486-490) ignore ctx (verified: clean encode(None)==encode(dirty), diff 0.0). A 'both'-regime rerun of this same battery WOULD incur the finding-19 offset (verified: both-regime encode(None)!=encode(dirty), max diff 1.13) and its numbers would not be comparable to the reported clean-regime numbers. Additionally note that distance() is feature-space L2 summed over 301x768 slots (verified), so these numbers are not on the symbolic edit-count scale and must never be compared numerically to copy floor 1.076 / tier-2 0.277.

sound_and_minor.title: Copy floor is encoder-matched, same-unit, and sees identical transitions; no rng-consumption divergence
sound_and_minor.corrected: No correction needed; the finding is confirmed as stated. Recommended caveats for the write-up (the finding already respects these, but they must be carried forward): (a) The comparison is valid ONLY within feature-space L2 units; the 63.13 floor and 66-72 arms must never be juxtaposed with the SYMBOLIC edit-count reference (copy 1.076, tier-2 0.277) as if on one scale. (b) "The arms are genuinely worse" is scoped to the state-changing h1 rollout clause specifically; on the calibration-AUC and goal-ranking clauses the learned arms do beat the copy floor (AUC 0.78-0.88 vs 0.50; rank_acc 0.77-0.87 vs 0.50), so the overall verdict on claim A is a rollout-clause result, not a blanket "arms add nothing." (c) The no-op/invalid=0 floor and the exact reproducibility both hinge on encoder determinism, which was verified to hold here but is an assumption to keep named.

sound_and_minor.title: The 'worse than copy' result is a REAL unchanged-slot haze effect, not a sum-vs-mean or cache-vs-live artifact
sound_and_minor.corrected: CONFIRMED. The 'worse than copy' result is a real unchanged-slot haze effect, not a sum-vs-mean or cache-vs-live artifact. Verified from source: battery distance = feature-space L2 per slot SUMMED over N_SLOTS=301 (models/gate2.py:499-500), copy_ratio_eval = per-slot L2 MEANED over 301 (gate2.py:249-250), so mean vs sum is a ratio-invariant 301x constant. Cache=live is bit-exact (max diff 0.0, 18 present slots, norm-sum 545.3151). On the train-monitor split the mean-metric reports the predictor BEATING copy (sc ratio 0.8515, noop 0.6577, inv 0.7120, all <1, reproduced from the checked-in ckpt across 4 seeds); on the pre-registered battery the SUM-metric reports the predictor LOSING to copy (5-seed sc pred 66.193 vs copy 63.127; per-seed ratio 1.046-1.051; frac pred<copy ~2%; reproduced bit-exactly from live encode). Decomposing the battery distance: on the ~1 changed slot the predictor ties/slightly beats copy, but on the ~300 unchanged slots it adds diffuse haze (e.g. at thr=5.0, UNCHANGED pred 36.16 > copy 33.01); the cleanest case is no-op/invalid transitions where the state is unchanged, copy is EXACTLY 0.0, and the predictor adds 5.06/5.63 of pure haze (matching the pretrained JSON). The MEAN dilutes this haze so the monitor looks like a win; the SUM surfaces it. Both metrics are self-consistent and the battery is the correct verdict metric. Direction is threshold-invariant and holds all 5 seeds. TWO WRITE-UP CORRECTIONS REQUIRED (not verdict-changing): (1) the dossier's illustrative digits (monitor 0.2458/0.2839/0.8657; battery 67.85/64.84; decomposition CHANGED 55.58/56.09, UNCHANGED 12.07/8.52) do NOT reproduce from the checked-in round4-pretrained ckpt (I get monitor 0.2468/0.2899/0.8515; no seed gives battery 67.85 with max 67.49; decomposition digits are threshold-dependent) — recompute the illustrative numbers from the checked-in artifacts and state the changed-slot threshold used; (2) the monitor-vs-battery divergence is dominated by MEAN-vs-SUM haze dilution but ALSO involves two secondary differences that should be named: copy_ratio_eval present-masks the anchor while the battery copy adapter uses the raw unmasked z0, and the monitor runs on the train-cache split vs the battery's live val-heldout trajectories.

sound_and_minor.title: No presence-norm rollout feedback, no foil survivorship, no goal-family skew, valid nets (identity guard + configs re-verified)
sound_and_minor.corrected: Harness-defect sweep (corrected): (2) Foil survivorship — CLEAN. All 25 seed-runs hit voe n=300/alt=300/two=300 with 0 dropped and 100 goal episodes; foils come from env.actions.sample_valid/sample_invalid (predictor-independent), so no pass/fail bias. (3) Goal-family skew — CLEAN. Round-robin stratification caps any single family at ~32% (max file_exists_with_class 32.1%); finding-19's ~90% skew is absent. (Note: the cited kind-n 150/75/156/180 are seed-0 pretrained-ew0 values; per-kind n varies across seeds, e.g. cwd_is 135-162, but balance holds.) (4) Net validity — VALID. Identity-at-init guard re-passes for all 4 configs (maxdiff=0.0); configs correct (pretrained/random init, 15.89M, step 3000, edit_weight 0 vs 10); all skipped=0; loss endpoints reproduce. Two process caveats: the loss is NOT monotone (only endpoints/copy_ratio trend down; intermediate loss oscillates), and the finding's 'AUC pre-ew0 seed0=0.7686' does not match the artifact's stored seed0 value 0.791857 — 0.7686 came from an incorrect fresh-rng call to calibration_eval; the artifact AUC 0.791857 is exactly reproducible when the battery rng order (rollout consumes rng before calibration) is respected. (1) Presence-norm feedback — NOT clean for all arms. The zero-crossing result (mean 0.0139, max 0.04, 0/~27600 crossing) holds only for the two edit_weight=0 arms (round4-pretrained, round4-randinit). The two edit_weight=10 arms spuriously activate absent slots: absent-at-encode slots cross presence_norm=1.0 during rollout — round4-pretrained-ew10 at 7/13/15 slots (h1/h2/h3, maxnorm ~12.4) and round4-randinit-ew10 (the top-scoring arm) at 8/9/18 (maxnorm ~29); full-val h1 for randinit-ew10 shows 71/1,320,059 crossings (0.0054%) spiking on dir slots to norm ~29. Because present=(feats.norm>presence_norm) is recomputed each rollout step (gate2.py:150), these spurious activations are treated as 'present' at the next horizon — the presence-norm rollout-feedback path is live for the ew10 nets. Impact is small (rare, bounded contribution to the summed distance) but the write-up must NOT claim 'no presence-norm feedback / never spuriously activates absent slots' across arms; it should state the zero-crossing measurement covered only the ew0 arms and report the nonzero ew10 crossings. Additionally the write-up should archive the identity-at-init guard output for all 4 runs (only round4-pretrained kept a train.log) or state it was re-derived post-hoc.

sound_and_minor.title: Pretrained-minus-randinit: two error clauses ARE statistically significant (not merely 'small'); framing needs precision
sound_and_minor.corrected: The finding is confirmed. Re-derived from the two battery JSONs: pretrained-minus-randinit (ew0) deltas are state_changing -0.619 (cstd 1.103, z -0.56, NS), noop -0.692 (cstd 0.172, z -4.03, SIG), invalid -2.298 (cstd 0.288, z -7.97, SIG), AUC -0.085 (cstd 0.024, z -3.58, SIG), rank_vs_random -0.040 (cstd 0.026, z -1.52, NS), rank_vs_invalid -0.014 (cstd 0.032, z -0.42, NS) — all matching the finding to the digit, with 'combined std' = sqrt(std_pre^2+std_rand^2) over the artifact's population stds. The suggested rewording is correct: use 'verdict-irrelevant (neither arm passes any clause; copyfloor state_changing=63.13 beats both ~66, and copy's noop/invalid=0.00 is unbeatable), and the two axes disagree — pretrained significantly reduces no-op/invalid haze while randinit has significantly higher calibration-AUC and ranking; both effects are real (~4-8 std) but neither rescues the gate.' Drop 'slightly' for invalid-haze and AUC. Two caveats to add: (1) the cstd z-score is an effect-size ratio, not the SEM of the difference (it omits the 1/sqrt(n) factor), so it is conservative — a proper Welch t makes the SIG labels stronger, not weaker; (2) state_changing is NS only under this unpaired cstd framing — a seed-paired test flags it significant-but-tiny (paired t=-10.2) since randinit's state-changing error is systematically ~0.6 higher per seed. Neither caveat changes any verdict.

sound_and_minor.title: All CLAIM B numbers reproduce exactly from the artifact JSONs
sound_and_minor.corrected: CLAIM B is correct: both gate-1 readouts FAIL the +20 architecture-matched-floor rule. Path-keyed linear macro_with_structural_exists = pretrained 100.0000% vs random-init 94.6467% (margin 5.3533 pct-pts, need +20) => FAIL; formula (cwd 1.0 + 1.0 + cls 1.0)/3 and (cwd 0.8394 + 1.0 + cls 1.0)/3, matching stored values bit-for-bit (frozen_probe.py:227; probe.json:82-93, probe-randominit.json:83-94). Pooled linear macro = pretrained 44.4053% vs random-init 30.6314% (margin 13.7739 pct-pts, need +20) => FAIL; formula (cwd + exists_balacc + cls_acc_given_exists)/3, matching stored values bit-for-bit (probe.py:158; probe.json:26-29, probe-randominit.json:27-30). Two additions the write-up must make: (1) the pre-registered rule has a SECOND clause — pretrained held-out linear macro >= recon twin + 10; recon twin pooled linear macro = 54.2434% (recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json:27), so pretrained 44.41% is 9.84 pct-pts BELOW the twin (fails the +10 clause harder than the floor clause) — cite both clause failures for a complete gate-1 verdict. (2) In the path-keyed readout, cls=1.0 and existence=1.0(structural) for both arms, so the entire 5.35 margin is the cwd gap (1.0 vs 0.8394); do not read it as broad world-state separation. Cosmetic: the finding's rounded restatement "(0.4564+0.7147+0.1611)/3=0.44405" actually yields 0.44407 from those rounded components; the exact full-precision value 0.44405 (reported) is correct.

sound_and_minor.title: Random-init encoder scores content-given-exists = 100%, cwd = 83.94% - 'readout not pretraining carries content' crux confirmed
sound_and_minor.corrected: Finding CONFIRMED. Random-init ModernBERT path-keyed content accuracy (cls_acc_given_exists) = 1.0 for both linear and MLP readouts and 1.0 at every depth {1:1.0, 2:1.0, 3:1.0} [probe-randominit.json:85,97 and 87-91,99-103]; existence is structurally hard-coded to 1.0 for both arms [frozen_probe.py:357]. Random-init path-keyed linear cwd = 0.83940 (83.94%) [probe-randominit.json:84] vs pretrained 1.000 [probe.json:83]. Therefore pretraining's only measurable path-keyed contribution is cwd (0.839 -> 1.000); content classification is carried entirely by the per-line readout, not by pretraining. The crux 'readout not pretraining carries content' holds. Required caveats for the write-up: (1) The content task is near-trivial by construction — env/render.py:32 prints the content class literally as a '[cK]' token (N_CONTENT=8) on each file line, and a single shared linear head over that per-line pooled vector separates the 8 classes even from random projections; state this as the reason the readout cannot discriminate pretraining. (2) These gate-1 probe artifacts are single-seed; label the 0.839->1.000 cwd gap as a single-seed observation, not a multi-seed verdict, per the project's multi-seed rule.

sound_and_minor.title: macro_with_structural_exists caps the achievable margin at +5.35 - the +20 rule is unsatisfiable on the path-keyed readout, so that 'FAIL' is vacuous
sound_and_minor.corrected: Confirmed. On the path-keyed readout, macro_with_structural_exists = (cwd + 1.0 + cls)/3 hard-codes existence=1.0 for every encoder (frozen_probe.py:227) and cls_acc is exactly 1.0 for both the pretrained arm and the random-init floor, so the ONLY term that can move is cwd (floor 0.83940). The maximum achievable pretrained-minus-floor margin is therefore (1.0 - 0.83940)/3 = 5.35 pts even for a perfect encoder; the +20 rule is structurally unattainable on path-keyed and 'path-keyed FAILS +20' is arithmetically true but vacuous. The write-up must state this explicitly and must NOT imply that a stronger encoder could have passed path-keyed. The meaningful gate-1 verdict rests on the POOLED linear readout: margin = 0.44405 - 0.30631 = +13.77 pts < +20 => FAIL, and pretrained (0.4441) is actually BELOW the reconstruction twin (0.5424), so it also fails the twin+10 clause. The learned-only path-keyed macro (cwd+cls)/2 gives +8.03 < +20, corroborating. Caveat the write-up should add: the pooled 13.77 spread is a composite-macro spread, not solely attributable to learned existence (pooled cwd also moves substantially, pre 0.456 vs rnd 0.152), so describe it as 'a genuine ~14pt composite spread where existence is learned (0.71 vs 0.60)' rather than implying existence alone drives it.

sound_and_minor.title: Matched floor is genuinely architecture-matched (config/params/budget/split/seed) and structural-existence counters are 0
sound_and_minor.corrected: Confirmed, no correction to claim B. The matched floor is genuinely architecture-matched: all 8 serialized config fields (model, model_params=149,014,272, data/v1, regime=both, eval_split=val-heldout-layouts, fit_states=20000, eval_states=5000, encoder_d_out=768), the probe_fit dict, and content pair counts (150000/73381) are identical between probe.json and probe-randominit.json; the floor is built via AutoModel.from_config(AutoConfig.from_pretrained(model)) under torch.manual_seed(seed) (frozen_probe.py:300-305) with random_init=true only on the floor; both default seed=0. Structural-existence mismatch counters are 0 for fit and eval on BOTH runs (probe.json:77-78, probe-randominit.json:78-79), so the +1.0 existence term in macro_with_structural_exists is verified against ground truth (frozen_probe.py:164-167, 357), not assumed, and cancels in the pretrained-minus-floor delta. Arithmetic checks: pretrained structural-macro (1.0+1.0+1.0)/3=1.0; floor (0.8394+1.0+1.0)/3=0.94647; margins are +5.35 pts (path-keyed structural-macro) and +13.77 pts (pooled linear macro), each recomputed exactly. REQUIRED caveat: gate-1 is a single-seed (seed 0) comparison across floor, pretrained, and recon twin, so both margins carry no error bars, unlike the 5-seed dynamics battery. (Not part of claim B, but the write-up must not overstate gate-1: the pre-registered pooled rule fails - 44.41 < floor+20=50.63 and < recon-twin+10=64.24.)

sound_and_minor.title: The +20/+10 rule references 'linear macro' but the two readouts use different macro definitions; twin clause is only evaluable on pooled
sound_and_minor.corrected: The pre-registered gate-1 rule references a single "held-out linear macro," but CLAIM B applies it to two non-comparable readouts. Pooled `macro` (probes/probe.py:158) = (cwd_acc + exists_balacc + cls_acc_given_exists)/3 with LEARNED existence; path-keyed `macro_with_structural_exists` (probes/frozen_probe.py:227) = (cwd_acc + 1.0 + cls_acc)/3 with existence HARD-PINNED to 1.0. The +20 floor clause fails on BOTH readouts: path-keyed +5.35 (100.00 vs randinit 94.65) and pooled +13.77 (44.41 vs randinit 30.63). The +10 twin clause is evaluable only on the comparable pooled readout, where it fails at -9.84 (pretrained pooled 44.41 vs twin 54.24 — the arm is BELOW the twin); the reconstruction twin (runs/recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json) has no path_keyed block and encoder_d_out=256, so no path-keyed twin comparator exists. The draft should (a) not conflate the two macro definitions under one "+20" number, (b) state the twin is pooled-only and additionally narrower (d=256 vs 768), and (c) flag these as single-seed linear-head numbers. The gate-1 FAIL conclusion is robust: every evaluable clause fails.

sound_and_minor.title: Recon-twin pooled macro 0.5424 and the +10-over-twin clause failure are correct and apples-to-apples
sound_and_minor.corrected: The recon-twin finding is CONFIRMED. Recon-twin pooled/linear macro = 0.5424339349 = 54.24% and the gate-1 +10-over-twin clause failure are correct and recompute exactly from components via probes/probe.py:158. All four macros verify to full precision: recon 0.5424339349, frozen 0.4440526913, sigreg 0.2602922122, untrained 0.2817053199. Ordering recon(54.24) > frozen(44.41) > JEPA arms(26.03/28.17) holds. Delta recon-frozen = +9.84 points (state as +9.84, not +10/~10). The +10 clause requires frozen >= twin+0.10 = 64.24%; frozen (44.41%) fails by -0.1984 because it sits BELOW the twin (sign inverted, not a margin miss). MLP direction matches (recon 0.5599 > frozen 0.4009). The comparison is fair on shared regime='both'/eval_split='val-heldout-layouts'/probe_fit; recon and sigreg twins are matched (seed=0/steps=4000/data-v1/encoder=cls, differing only in arm). REQUIRED CAVEATS for the write-up: (1) state the delta as +9.84 points, not +10; (2) note that this is the LINEAR pass-bar readout; (3) acknowledge the twins use CLS d_out=256 while frozen uses pooled d_out=768 — a dimensionality/readout mismatch that cuts against frozen and therefore strengthens rather than weakens the conclusion; (4) minor provenance note: sigreg's config header was not stored in its logs (inferred from run name and pred+reg loss structure), whereas recon's config is fully stored.

sound_and_minor.title: Recon twin eval is on genuinely held-out disjoint layouts; high macro is not a seen-split artifact
sound_and_minor.corrected: Confirmed. The recon-twin eval is on genuinely held-out, disjoint layouts and 54.24% (linear macro) is a real held-out generalization number, not a seen-split artifact. Re-derived: manifest train/val pools = 1566/434 layouts, intersection 0; and (stronger) the actual sampled files data/v1/train.jsonl (1377 distinct layouts) and data/v1/val.jsonl (249 distinct layouts) are strictly disjoint (intersection 0), with 0 val-file layouts in the manifest train pool. probe-v2.json: eval_split='val-heldout-layouts', dropped_unseen=0, fit_states=20000, eval_states=5000, probe_fit.steps=400, linear.macro=0.5424 (54.24%). Per probes/probe.py:241-267, dropped_unseen=0 is a HARDCODED constant on the default (non --seen-layouts) path that loads val.jsonl as a separate file; the drop filter (251-254) applies only to the opt-in --seen-layouts diagnostic. Provenance: runs/run_step5.sh:18-19 runs probes.probe --data data/v1 with no --seen-layouts flag. So dropped_unseen=0 is expected and is not a sign of a skipped held-out filter. Two write-up notes: (a) cite the producing script as probes/probe.py (not probes/frozen_probe.py); (b) 1566/434 are manifest POOL sizes — the distinct layouts actually realized in the sampled train/val files are 1377/249 (sampling with replacement), still disjoint.

sound_and_minor.title: Recon twin suppresses the banner nuisance (0.0204, ~chance) whereas frozen leaks it (0.451); a caveat the twin comparison should note
sound_and_minor.corrected: CONFIRMED. Recon twin suppresses the banner nuisance: pooled-linear banner_acc = 0.0204 (runs/recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json:28), i.e. 1.31x the 64-way chance of 0.015625 — essentially at chance, so the recon encoder does NOT encode banner identity. sigreg is even lower at 0.0126 (0.81x chance, below chance; runs/sigreg-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json:28), and untrained-cls is 0.2242 (14.3x chance; runs/untrained-cls-v1-v2.json:28). By contrast the FROZEN ModernBERT pooled-linear features leak banner heavily: banner_acc = 0.451 (28.9x chance; runs/frozen-modernbert-v1/probe.json:30) — and frozen leaks across every arm (path_keyed linear 0.3092, path_keyed mlp 0.2274, pooled mlp 0.3384). The banner_swap dz-ratio corroborates state-sensitivity/banner-insensitivity: recon 0.14123 (state dz 7.905 >> swap dz 1.116), sigreg 0.07544, untrained 1.35774. Because macro = (cwd + exists + cls)/3 (probes/probe.py:158) explicitly EXCLUDES banner_acc, this nuisance difference does not distort the macro comparison and does not change the +10-clause verdict. Suggested caveat is accurate and should be added, with two clarifications: (1) the 0.0204 vs 0.451 comparison is a fair pooled-linear-vs-pooled-linear contrast (both readouts are pooled linear), and the frozen leak holds regardless of readout arm; (2) recon's 0.0204 is technically 1.31x chance (genuinely at-chance for a 64-way task), and sigreg at 0.0126 is actually below chance.

sound_and_minor.title: Core arithmetic, unit-matching, and the edit-weight/haze direction all check out — with required caveats on units, the untested A3/novel-noise dimensions, and the knife-edge AUC
sound_and_minor.corrected: FINDING CONFIRMED (sound). Independently re-derived from artifacts/code with PYTHONHASHSEED=0: (1) all 2x2 means reproduce exactly as MEAN-of-per-seed aggregates — copyfloor 63.127/0.000/0.000/0.500, pretrained ew0 66.193/5.097/5.547/0.784, randinit ew0 66.812/5.789/7.845/0.869, pretrained ew10 69.086/10.327/17.020/0.869, randinit ew10 72.183/12.048/22.256/0.879. (2) Unit-matching is correct: Gate2Adapter.encode uses the FROZEN ModernBERT LM (gate2.py:479, not self.net), CopyDynamics predict=identity delegates distance to base, and distance = sum-over-301-slots of per-slot 768-d L2 (verified empirically, not flat-L2, not mean) — so 63.13 is a genuine same-representation same-distance floor shared by ALL five arms, and every learned arm is worse than copy on state-changing while hazing on no-ops (FAIL robust in direction). Symbolic 1.076/0.277 use edit-count units (OracleAdapter.distance, dynamics.py:59) and are correctly flagged as non-comparable. (3) Claim B exact: path-keyed floor macro 94.647% (margin +5.353 < 20 FAIL); content-given-exists 100% for BOTH pretrained and random-init path-keyed (0.0 margin -> readout, not pretraining, carries content); pretraining's path-keyed contribution is cwd 83.94%->100% (+16.06); pooled 44.405 vs 30.631 = +13.774 < 20 FAIL. (4) 'Higher edit-weight worsens haze' holds monotonically within {0,10}. Keep the FAIL verdicts. Carry all six required caveats verbatim: (a) edit-weight swept only {0,10}, round-2 default was 100 (gate2.py:517) — a 'no calibration sweet spot' claim would over-generalize; data supports only 'within {0,10}, higher ew worsens haze'; (b) A3/nuisance UNTESTED for the feature-space arm — every round-4 battery is adapter=gate2-clean, NO input-regime=both battery exists (task #4c 'completed' notwithstanding), the input_regime='both' code path is runnable but the delta was never produced; finding-19's tautology caveat applies, A3 remains open; (c) novel-noise untested — noise_seed = within-split index, held-out eval replays train's noise streams (status line 106); (d) seen-vs-held-out satisfied — eval_split='val-heldout-layouts, val-heldout-predicates' verified; (e) goal-family skew fixed — round-robin stratified over 4 predicate kinds (uneven n's 150/75/156/180 but the ~90%-one-family skew is gone), a genuine improvement to credit; (f) AUC 0.9 bar knife-edge — global max single-seed 0.8998741 (randinit_ew10 seed 1) < 0.9 by 0.000126, rounds to 0.900. TWO ADDITIONS a write-up should carry (verdict unchanged): the matched-floor number in status finding-14 (cwd 42% / macro >=80.7% / margin <=+19.3) is superseded by the hardened step-5a re-run (cwd 83.94% / macro 94.647% / margin +5.35); and the pooled clause ALSO fails the recon-twin +10 sub-clause (pretrained pooled 44.405 is BELOW the 256-d recon twin 54.243, margin -9.838), which is cross-dimension (768 vs 256, not strictly architecture-matched) but pretraining loses to it anyway, reinforcing the FAIL.
